title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Johann D. Gaebler (2026). wstats: Weighted Descriptive Statistics. R package version 0.0.0.9000.
code: @Manual{,   title = {wstats: Weighted Descriptive Statistics},   author = {{Johann D. Gaebler}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000}, }

title: Weighted Descriptive Statistics
text: Base R provides weighted.mean() nothing else. wstats fills gap weighted versions common descriptive statistics, targeting importance-weight use case (Bayesian bootstrap, importance sampling).

title: Weighted Descriptive Statistics
code: library(wstats)  x <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5) w <- c(0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 1.0, 0.5)   # unnormalised importance weights  weighted_var(x, w) weighted_sd(x, w) weighted_quantile(x, w, probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) weighted_median(x, w) weighted_mad(x, w) weighted_skewness(x, w) weighted_kurtosis(x, w)  y <- c(2, 3, 1, 5, 4) weighted_cov(x, y, w) weighted_cor(x, y, w)

title: Weighted Descriptive Statistics
text: functions use population (importance-weight) formula — weights treated probability masses discrete distribution, survey sampling weights. Concretely, variance Σ(ŵᵢ (xᵢ − μ)²) ŵᵢ = wᵢ / Σwⱼ, Bessel correction. right formula Bayesian bootstrap importance sampling; bias-corrected version survey weights may added future release. Computationally intensive routines implemented C++ via cpp11.

title: Weighted correlation — weighted_cor
text: Weighted correlation coefficient, computed weighted_cov(x, y, w) / (weighted_sd(x, w) * weighted_sd(y, w)).

title: Weighted correlation — weighted_cor
text: x numeric vector observations. y numeric vector observations (length x). w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, observations NA x, y, w removed computation.

title: Weighted correlation — weighted_cor
text: single numeric value [-1, 1].

title: Weighted covariance — weighted_cov
text: Computes population (importance-weight) weighted covariance sum(w_hat * (x - mu_x) * (y - mu_y)).

title: Weighted covariance — weighted_cov
text: x numeric vector observations. y numeric vector observations (length x). w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, observations NA x, y, w removed computation.

title: Weighted covariance — weighted_cov
text: single numeric value.

title: Weighted excess kurtosis — weighted_kurtosis
text: Computes population weighted excess kurtosis: sum(w_hat * ((x - mu) / sigma)^4) - 3.

title: Weighted excess kurtosis — weighted_kurtosis
text: x numeric vector observations. w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, paired NAs x w removed computation. FALSE (default) NA present, NA returned.

title: Weighted excess kurtosis — weighted_kurtosis
text: single numeric value.

title: Weighted median absolute deviation — weighted_mad
text: Computes weighted MAD weighted median |x - median(x, w)|, scaled constant (default 1.4826 consistency stats::mad() normality).

title: Weighted median absolute deviation — weighted_mad
text: x numeric vector observations. w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, paired NAs x w removed computation. FALSE (default) NA present, NA returned. constant Scale factor (default 1.4826).

title: Weighted median absolute deviation — weighted_mad
text: single numeric value.

title: Weighted median — weighted_median
text: Convenience wrapper: weighted_quantile(x, w, 0.5, na.rm).

title: Weighted median — weighted_median
text: x numeric vector observations. w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, paired NAs x w removed computation. FALSE (default) NA present, NA returned.

title: Weighted median — weighted_median
text: single numeric value.

title: Weighted quantiles — weighted_quantile
text: Computes weighted quantiles using type-7 analog: observations placed midpoints weight intervals cumulative weight distribution, rescaled [0, 1], quantiles obtained linear interpolation. equal weights matches quantile(x, type = 7).

title: Weighted quantiles — weighted_quantile
code: weighted_quantile(x, w, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = FALSE)

title: Weighted quantiles — weighted_quantile
text: x numeric vector observations. w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). probs numeric vector probabilities [0, 1]. na.rm Logical. TRUE, paired NAs x w removed.

title: Weighted quantiles — weighted_quantile
text: numeric vector length probs.

title: Weighted standard deviation — weighted_sd
text: Square root weighted_var().

title: Weighted standard deviation — weighted_sd
text: x numeric vector observations. w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, paired NAs x w removed computation. FALSE (default) NA present, NA returned.

title: Weighted standard deviation — weighted_sd
text: single numeric value.

title: Weighted skewness — weighted_skewness
text: Computes population weighted skewness (Fisher's g1): sum(w_hat * ((x - mu) / sigma)^3).

title: Weighted skewness — weighted_skewness
text: x numeric vector observations. w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, paired NAs x w removed computation. FALSE (default) NA present, NA returned.

title: Weighted skewness — weighted_skewness
text: single numeric value.

title: Weighted variance — weighted_var
text: Computes population (importance-weight) weighted variance sum(w_hat * (x - mu)^2) w_hat = w / sum(w) mu = weighted.mean(x, w).

title: Weighted variance — weighted_var
text: x numeric vector observations. w numeric vector non-negative weights (need sum 1). na.rm Logical. TRUE, paired NAs x w removed computation. FALSE (default) NA present, NA returned.

title: Weighted variance — weighted_var
text: single numeric value.

title: wstats: Weighted Descriptive Statistics — wstats-package
text: Weighted versions common descriptive statistics (variance, standard deviation, covariance, correlation, quantiles) importance weights. Computationally intensive routines implemented C++ via cpp11.

title: wstats: Weighted Descriptive Statistics — wstats-package
text: Maintainer: Johann D. Gaebler @jgaeb.com
